summary: Docker-based reproduction bundle designed to demonstrate that POST /api/v1/chart/data accepts requests without CSRF token validation when using cookie-based authentication, confirming the CSRF exemption vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: A POST request to /api/v1/chart/data without the X-CSRFToken header should be accepted (not rejected with 400/403) when using an authenticated session, confirming that the CSRF exemption allows cross-origin request forgery.
actual_observed_result: PoC not yet executed. The reproduction bundle is complete and self-contained.
verification_method: The exploit script (exploit.py) authenticates to Superset, obtains a session cookie, then sends a POST request to /api/v1/chart/data WITHOUT the X-CSRFToken header. Success is verified by the server accepting the request (non-403/400 response to the CSRF-exempt endpoint) while potentially rejecting similar requests to non-exempt endpoints. The script emits 'proof_marker=EVOHUNT_CSRF_BYPASS_CONFIRMED' on success.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires installing Superset from source which takes 10-20 minutes for pip dependency installation
The PoC demonstrates CSRF exemption by showing requests without CSRF tokens are accepted; a real CSRF attack would use a browser on a different origin

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Apache Superset repository from GitHub (shallow clone of master branch), checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts the checkout into the container at /target-repo.
